preview_text:
The model favors the Netherlands at 69%, with Tunisia given a 9% chance of victory. The underdog's low win probability reflects a substantial gap in expected strength, though a draw carries near-one-in-five likelihood.

The outcome distribution shows the Netherlands as clear favorite but not overwhelming favorite. Tunisia's 9% win probability paired with a 22% draw chance suggests the model sees a competitive match that could produce an upset or a stalemate, rather than one tilted decisively toward a Netherlands victory from kickoff. The 61-percentage-point gap between the two win probabilities indicates asymmetry, but the draw probability's relative size signals meaningful competitive tension.

The model and market are broadly aligned. The Netherlands advantage sits at 69–72%, a modest 3-percentage-point divergence. Polymarket prices cluster similarly. The main discrepancy is draw probability: the model assigns 22%, while the sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both estimate around 16–17%. The dashboard takes no stance on which source is correct.
